key_insight: Adaptive superpixels can compress 2D diffraction data while preserving high-information features and symmetry, making downstream analysis more interpretable and efficient.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical preprocessing strategy for large 4D-STEM and synchrotron diffraction datasets, reducing computation and accelerating materials phase mapping.
method: Group neighboring detector pixels into variable-size superpixels based on information content
Compare two variance-minimizing schemes: K-means clustering (top-down) and agglomerative clustering (bottom-up)
Accelerate superpixel construction with Gaussian random projection before distance-based clustering
evidence_signal: The abstract reports superior scaling and interpretability for agglomerative clustering and over 100-fold acceleration for NMF-based phase mapping on a 4D-STEM dataset using superpixels.
watch_item: Because the method is distance-based, performance may depend on projection quality, clustering choices, and how well the superpixel layout matches the underlying diffraction symmetries.
paper_title: Using superpixels for interpretable feature reduction in large 2D diffraction datasets
